parts.rangeLabel: Read the text and answer questions 1–13

passage.text: The history of the world’s most luxurious fabric, from ancient China to the present day

Silk is a fine, smooth material produced from the cocoons – soft protective shells – that are made by mulberry silkworms (insect larvae). Legend has it that it was Lei Tzu, wife of the Yellow Emperor, ruler of China in about 3000 BC, who discovered silkworms. One account of the story goes that as she was taking a walk in her husband’s gardens, she discovered that silkworms were responsible for the destruction of several mulberry trees. She collected a number of cocoons and sat down to have a rest. It just so happened that while she was sipping some tea, one of the cocoons that she had collected landed in the hot tea and started to unravel into a fine thread. Lei Tzu found that she could wind this thread around her fingers. Subsequently, she persuaded her husband to allow her to rear silkworms on a grove of mulberry trees. She also devised a special reel to draw the fibres from the cocoon into a single thread so that they would be strong enough to be woven into fabric. While it is unknown just how much of this is true, it is certainly known that silk cultivation has existed in China for several millennia.

Originally, silkworm farming was solely restricted to women, and it was they who were responsible for the growing, harvesting and weaving. Silk quickly grew into a symbol of status, and originally, only royalty were entitled to have clothes made of silk. The rules were gradually relaxed over the years until finally during the Qing Dynasty (1644—1911 AD), even peasants, the lowest caste, were also entitled to wear silk. Sometime during the Han Dynasty (206 BC-220 AD), silk was so prized that it was also used as a unit of currency. Government officials were paid their salary in silk, and farmers paid their taxes in grain and silk. Silk was also used as diplomatic gifts by the emperor. Fishing lines, bowstrings, musical instruments and paper were all made using silk. The earliest indication of silk paper being used was discovered in the tomb of a noble who is estimated to have died around 168 AD.

Demand for this exotic fabric eventually created the lucrative trade route now known as the Silk Road, taking silk westward and bringing gold, silver and wool to the East. It was named the Silk Road after its most precious commodity, which was considered to be worth more than gold. The Silk Road stretched over 6,000 kilometres from Eastern China to the Mediterranean Sea, following the Great Wall of China, climbing the Pamir mountain range, crossing modern-day Afghanistan and going on to the Middle East, with a major trading market in Damascus. From there, the merchandise was shipped across the Mediterranean Sea. Few merchants travelled the entire route; goods were handled mostly by a series of middlemen.

With the mulberry silkworm being native to China, the country was the world’s sole producer of silk for many hundreds of years. The secret of silk-making eventually reached the rest of the world via the Byzantine Empire, which ruled over the Mediterranean region of southern Europe, North Africa and the Middle East during the period 330—1453 AD. According to another legend, monks working for the Byzantine emperor Justinian smuggle silkworm eggs to Constantinople (Istanbul in modern-day Turkey) in 550 AD, concealed inside hollow bamboo walking canes. The Byzantines were as secretive as the Chinese, however, and for many centuries the weaving and trading of silk fabric was a strict imperial monopoly. Then in the seventh century, the Arabs conquered Persia, capturing their magnificent silks in the process.

Silk production thus spread through Africa, Sicily and Spain as the Arabs swept, through these lands. Andalusia in southern Spain was Europe’s main silk-producing centre in the tenth century. By the thirteenth century, however, Italy had become Europe’s leader in silk production and export. Venetian merchants traded extensively in silk and encouraged silk growers to settle in Italy. Even now, silk processed in the province of Como in northern Italy enjoys an esteemed reputation.

The nineteenth century and industrialisation saw the downfall of the European silk industry. Cheaper Japanese silk, trade in which was greatly facilitated by the opening of the Suez Canal, was one of the many factors driving the trend. Then in the twentieth century, new manmade fibres, such as nylon, started to be used in what had traditionally been silk products, such as stockings and parachutes. The two world wars, which interrupted the supply of raw material from Japan, also stifled the European silk industry. After the Second World War, Japan’s silk production was restored, with improved production and quality of raw silk. Japan was to remain the world’s biggest producer of raw silk, and practically the only major exporter of raw silk, until the 1970s. However, in more recent decades, China has gradually recaptured its position as the world’s biggest producer and exporter of raw silk and silk yarn. Today, around 125,000 metric tons of silk are produced in the world, and almost two thirds of that production takes place in China.

parts.passageHtml:
The story of silk

The history of the world’s most luxurious fabric, from ancient China to the present day

Silk is a fine, smooth material produced from the cocoons – soft protective shells – that are made by mulberry silkworms (insect larvae). Legend has it that it was Lei Tzu, wife of the Yellow Emperor, ruler of China in about 3000 BC, who discovered silkworms. One account of the story goes that as she was taking a walk in her husband’s gardens, she discovered that silkworms were responsible for the destruction of several mulberry trees. She collected a number of cocoons and sat down to have a rest. It just so happened that while she was sipping some tea, one of the cocoons that she had collected landed in the hot tea and started to unravel into a fine thread. Lei Tzu found that she could wind this thread around her fingers. Subsequently, she persuaded her husband to allow her to rear silkworms on a grove of mulberry trees. She also devised a special reel to draw the fibres from the cocoon into a single thread so that they would be strong enough to be woven into fabric. While it is unknown just how much of this is true, it is certainly known that silk cultivation has existed in China for several millennia.

Originally, silkworm farming was solely restricted to women, and it was they who were responsible for the growing, harvesting and weaving. Silk quickly grew into a symbol of status, and originally, only royalty were entitled to have clothes made of silk. The rules were gradually relaxed over the years until finally during the Qing Dynasty (1644—1911 AD), even peasants, the lowest caste, were also entitled to wear silk. Sometime during the Han Dynasty (206 BC-220 AD), silk was so prized that it was also used as a unit of currency. Government officials were paid their salary in silk, and farmers paid their taxes in grain and silk. Silk was also used as diplomatic gifts by the emperor. Fishing lines, bowstrings, musical instruments and paper were all made using silk. The earliest indication of silk paper being used was discovered in the tomb of a noble who is estimated to have died around 168 AD.

Demand for this exotic fabric eventually created the lucrative trade route now known as the Silk Road, taking silk westward and bringing gold, silver and wool to the East. It was named the Silk Road after its most precious commodity, which was considered to be worth more than gold. The Silk Road stretched over 6,000 kilometres from Eastern China to the Mediterranean Sea, following the Great Wall of China, climbing the Pamir mountain range, crossing modern-day Afghanistan and going on to the Middle East, with a major trading market in Damascus. From there, the merchandise was shipped across the Mediterranean Sea. Few merchants travelled the entire route; goods were handled mostly by a series of middlemen.

With the mulberry silkworm being native to China, the country was the world’s sole producer of silk for many hundreds of years. The secret of silk-making eventually reached the rest of the world via the Byzantine Empire, which ruled over the Mediterranean region of southern Europe, North Africa and the Middle East during the period 330—1453 AD. According to another legend, monks working for the Byzantine emperor Justinian smuggle silkworm eggs to Constantinople (Istanbul in modern-day Turkey) in 550 AD, concealed inside hollow bamboo walking canes. The Byzantines were as secretive as the Chinese, however, and for many centuries the weaving and trading of silk fabric was a strict imperial monopoly. Then in the seventh century, the Arabs conquered Persia, capturing their magnificent silks in the process.

Silk production thus spread through Africa, Sicily and Spain as the Arabs swept, through these lands. Andalusia in southern Spain was Europe’s main silk-producing centre in the tenth century. By the thirteenth century, however, Italy had become Europe’s leader in silk production and export. Venetian merchants traded extensively in silk and encouraged silk growers to settle in Italy. Even now, silk processed in the province of Como in northern Italy enjoys an esteemed reputation.

The nineteenth century and industrialisation saw the downfall of the European silk industry. Cheaper Japanese silk, trade in which was greatly facilitated by the opening of the Suez Canal, was one of the many factors driving the trend. Then in the twentieth century, new manmade fibres, such as nylon, started to be used in what had traditionally been silk products, such as stockings and parachutes. The two world wars, which interrupted the supply of raw material from Japan, also stifled the European silk industry. After the Second World War, Japan’s silk production was restored, with improved production and quality of raw silk. Japan was to remain the world’s biggest producer of raw silk, and practically the only major exporter of raw silk, until the 1970s. However, in more recent decades, China has gradually recaptured its position as the world’s biggest producer and exporter of raw silk and silk yarn. Today, around 125,000 metric tons of silk are produced in the world, and almost two thirds of that production takes place in China.

questionGroups.instruction: Questions 1–9Complete the notes below.Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
THE STORY OF SILK

Early silk production in China

  • Around 3000 BC, according to legend:

– silkworm cocoon fell into emperor’s wife’s 1_______

– emperor’s wife invented a 2_______ to pull out silk fibres

  • Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
  • Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
  • Silk used as a form of 5_______

– e.g. farmers’ taxes consisted partly of silk

  • Silk used for many purposes

– e.g. evidence found of 6_______ made from silk around 168 AD

Silk reaches rest of world

  • Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
  • 550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
  • Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
  • 20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production
questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 1–9

Complete the notes below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

listOptions.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questionGroups.noteContent:
**Early silk production in China**
• Around 3000 BC, according to legend:
– silkworm cocoon fell into emperor’s wife’s [1]
–  emperor’s wife invented a [2] to pull out silk fibres
• Only [3] were allowed to produce silk
• Only [4] were allowed to wear silk
• Silk used as a form of [5]
– e.g. farmers’ taxes consisted partly of silk
• Silk used for many purposes
– e.g. evidence found of [6] made from silk around 168 AD
**Silk reaches rest of world**
• Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back [7] and precious metals
• 550 AD: [8] hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
• Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
• 20th century: [9] and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.prompt:
silkworm cocoon fell into emperor’s wife’s 
 1. ________

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Around 3000 BC”, “legend”, “cocoon fell into”, “emperor’s wife’s”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “...while she was sipping some tea, one of the cocoons that she had collected landed in the hot tea and started to unravel...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Trong văn bản gốc, chiếc kén (cocoon) đã rơi trúng (landed in) vào cốc trà nóng của người vợ hoàng đế khi bà đang uống nước. Do đó, danh từ chỉ nơi kén rơi vào chính là tách trà.

=> Đáp án: tea
questions.questionHtml:
THE STORY OF SILK

Early silk production in China

  • Around 3000 BC, according to legend:

– silkworm cocoon fell into emperor’s wife’s 1_______

– emperor’s wife invented a 2_______ to pull out silk fibres

  • Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
  • Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
  • Silk used as a form of 5_______

– e.g. farmers’ taxes consisted partly of silk

  • Silk used for many purposes

– e.g. evidence found of 6_______ made from silk around 168 AD

Silk reaches rest of world

  • Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
  • 550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
  • Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
  • 20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.prompt: –  emperor’s wife invented a  2. ________  to pull out silk fibres

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “invented” (phát minh/sáng chế), “pull out silk fibres” (kéo các sợi tơ ra)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “She also devised a special reel to draw the fibres from the cocoon into a single thread...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “devised” đồng nghĩa với “invented”, và cụm “draw the fibres from the cocoon” tương đồng với “pull out silk fibres”. Công cụ mà bà sáng chế ra để thực hiện việc này là một chiếc guồng quay tơ.

=> Đáp án: reel

questions.prompt: Only 3 _______ were allowed to produce silk

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Only” (chỉ duy nhất), “allowed to produce silk” (được phép sản xuất lụa)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2: “Originally, silkworm farming was solely restricted to women, and it was they who were responsible for the growing, harvesting and weaving.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Trích dẫn chỉ rõ ban đầu việc nuôi tằm (silkworm farming/silk production) chỉ bị giới hạn độc quyền cho một đối tượng duy nhất (solely restricted to = only allowed to). Đối tượng đó chính là phụ nữ.

=> Đáp án: women

questions.prompt: Only 4 _______ were allowed to wear silk

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Only”, “allowed to wear silk” (được phép mặc đồ lụa)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2: “...and originally, only royalty were entitled to have clothes made of silk.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “entitled to have clothes made of silk” (có đặc quyền sở hữu quần áo làm từ lụa) đồng nghĩa với cụm “allowed to wear silk”. Nhóm người duy nhất có quyền lợi này vào thời kỳ đầu chính là hoàng tộc.

=> Đáp án: royalty

questions.prompt: Silk used as a form of 5 _______ e.g. farmers’ taxes consisted partly of silk

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “used as a form of” (được dùng như một hình thức...), “farmers’ taxes”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2: “...silk was so prized that it was also used as a unit of currency. Government officials were paid their salary in silk, and farmers paid their taxes in grain and silk.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ ra lụa có giá trị lớn tới mức được sử dụng làm một đơn vị tiền tệ, minh chứng bằng việc trả lương cho quan lại hay thu thuế của nông dân bằng lụa. Do đó, lụa đóng vai trò như một hình thức tiền tệ.

=> Đáp án: currency

questions.prompt: Silk used for many purposes e.g. evidence found of 6 _______ made from silk around 168 AD

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “evidence found of”, “made from silk”, “around 168 AD”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2: “The earliest indication of silk paper being used was discovered in the tomb of a noble who is estimated to have died around 168 AD.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “evidence found” tương ứng với cụm “indication... was discovered”. Loại vật phẩm làm từ lụa được tìm thấy trong ngôi mộ có niên đại quanh năm 168 SCN chính là giấy lụa. Vì đề bài yêu cầu chọn MỘT TỪ DUY NHẤT, ta giữ lại danh từ chính.

=> Đáp án: paper

questions.prompt: Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7 _______ and precious metals

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Silk Road”, “take silk westward”, “bring back”, “precious metals” (kim loại quý)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: “...taking silk westward and bringing gold, silver and wool to the East.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Việc mang lụa sang phương Tây giúp các thương nhân thu về (bringing back) vàng, bạc và len cho phương Đông. Vàng và bạc đã được đề bài tóm gọn bằng cụm “precious metals”, vì thế danh từ còn thiếu ở đây là len.

=> Đáp án: wool

questions.prompt: 550 AD:  8. ________hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “550 AD”, “hide” (giấu), “silkworm eggs in canes”, “Constantinople”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: “...monks working for the Byzantine emperor Justinian smuggled silkworm eggs to Constantinople... in 550 AD, concealed inside hollow bamboo walking canes.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “hide” đồng nghĩa với từ “concealed” và “smuggled”. Những người đã thực hiện hành vi lén lút giấu trứng tằm vào gậy tre để đưa tới vùng Constantinople vào năm 550 SCN là các nhà sư.

=> Đáp án: monks

questions.prompt: 20th century:  9.  ________ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “20th century”, “manmade fibres” (sợi nhân tạo), “cause decline” (gây ra sự suy giảm)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “Then in the twentieth century, new manmade fibres, such as nylon, started to be used in what had traditionally been silk products...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Vào thế kỷ 20, các loại sợi nhân tạo mới xuất hiện đã thay thế vị trí của lụa trong các mặt hàng truyền thống, dẫn đến sự suy yếu của ngành này. Tên loại sợi nhân tạo tiêu biểu được bài viết liệt kê làm ví dụ là ni-lông.

=> Đáp án: nylon

questions.prompt: Question 9

options.label: Only 3_______ were allowed to produce silk
Only 4_______ were allowed to wear silk
Silk used as a form of 5_______
Merchants use Silk Road to take silk westward and bring back 7_______ and precious metals
550 AD: 8_______ hide silkworm eggs in canes and take them to Constantinople
Silk production spreads across Middle East and Europe
20th century: 9_______ and other manmade fibres cause decline in silk production

questionGroups.instruction: Questions 10–13Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?In boxes 10–13 on your answer sheet, writeTRUE if the statement agrees with the informationFALSE if the statement contradicts the informationNOT GIVEN if there is no information on this
questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 10–13

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 10–13 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questionGroups.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.prompt: Gold was the most valuable material transported along the Silk Road.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Gold”, “the most valuable material” (vật liệu có giá trị nhất), “Silk Road”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: “It was named the Silk Road after its most precious commodity, which was considered to be worth more than gold.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn khẳng định con đường được đặt tên theo mặt hàng quý giá nhất của nó (most precious commodity) chính là lụa, và lụa còn được coi là có giá trị hơn cả vàng (worth more than gold). Do đó, phát biểu nói "vàng là vật liệu quý giá nhất" hoàn toàn sai và mâu thuẫn với bài đọc.

=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.prompt: Most tradesmen only went along certain sections of the Silk Road.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Most tradesmen” (phần lớn thương nhân), “only went along certain sections” (chỉ đi dọc theo một vài đoạn nhất định)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: “Few merchants travelled the entire route; goods were handled mostly by a series of middlemen.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ ra rằng rất ít thương nhân đi hết toàn bộ con đường (Few merchants travelled the entire route), thay vào đó hàng hóa chủ yếu được chuyền tay qua một chuỗi các trung gian (middlemen). Điều này đồng nghĩa với việc phần lớn bọn họ chỉ di chuyển ở những cung chặng, phân đoạn nhất định.

=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: The Byzantines spread the practice of silk production across the West.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “The Byzantines”, “spread” (lan truyền/phổ biến), “across the West” (khắp phương Tây)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4 & Đoạn 5: “The Byzantines were as secretive as the Chinese, however, and for many centuries the weaving and trading of silk fabric was a strict imperial monopoly. Then in the seventh century, the Arabs conquered Persia... Silk production thus spread through Africa, Sicily and Spain as the Arabs swept through these lands.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài viết nêu rõ người Byzantine cực kỳ giữ bí mật (secretive) và duy trì sự độc quyền nghiêm ngặt của hoàng gia (strict imperial monopoly) chứ không hề chia sẻ ra ngoài. Việc nghề lụa được lan rộng khắp các vùng châu Phi, Sicily và Tây Ban Nha thực chất là nhờ dòng người Ả Rập (Arabs) đánh chiếm và quét qua các vùng đất này. Do đó câu khẳng định trên là sai.

=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.prompt: Silk yarn makes up the majority of silk currently exported from China.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Silk yarn” (sợi tơ lụa), “makes up the majority” (chiếm phần lớn/đại đa số), “currently exported from China”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “...China has gradually recaptured its position as the world’s biggest producer and exporter of raw silk and silk yarn. Today, around 125,000 metric tons of silk are produced in the world, and almost two thirds of that production takes place in China.”  

Bước 3: Phân tích: Thông tin trong đoạn chỉ khẳng định Trung Quốc đã lấy lại vị thế là nhà sản xuất và xuất khẩu lớn nhất thế giới về cả hai mặt hàng: lụa thô (raw silk) và sợi lụa (silk yarn). Bài viết hoàn toàn không cung cấp số liệu so sánh chi tiết để biết mặt hàng sợi lụa có phải chiếm "đại đa số" tổng lượng xuất khẩu hay không.

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

parts.rangeLabel: Read the text and answer questions 14–26

passage.text: Animal migration, however it is defined, is far more than just the movement of animals. It can loosely be described as travel that takes place at regular intervals – often in an annual cycle – that may involve many members of a species, and is rewarded only after a long journey. It suggests inherited instinct. The biologist Hugh Dingle has identified five characteristics that apply, in varying degrees and combinations, to all migrations. They are prolonged movements that carry animals outside familiar habitats; they tend to be linear, not zigzaggy; they involve special behaviours concerning preparation (such as overfeeding) and arrival; they demand special allocations of energy. And one more: migrating animals maintain an intense attentiveness to the greater mission, which keeps them undistracted by temptations and undeterred by challenges that would turn other animals aside.

An arctic tern, on its 20,000 km flight from the extreme south of South America to the Arctic circle, will take no notice of a nice smelly herring offered from a bird-watcher’s boat along the way. While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on. Why? The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of something we humans find admirable: larger purpose. In other words, it is determined to reach its destination. The bird senses that it can eat, rest and mate later. Right now it is totally focused on the journey; its undivided intent is arrival.

Reaching some gravelly coastline in the Arctic, upon which other arctic terns have converged, will serve its larger purpose as shaped by evolution: finding a place, a time, and a set of circumstances in which it can successfully hatch and rear offspring.

But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study. Joe! Berger, of the University of Montana, who works on the American pronghorn and other large terrestrial mammals, prefers what he calls a simple, practical definition suited to his beasts: ‘movements from a seasonal home area away to another home area and back again’. Generally the reason for such seasonal back-and-forth movement is to seek resources that aren’t available within a single area year-round.

But daily vertical movements by zooplankton in the ocean – upward by night to seek food, downward by day to escape predators – can also be considered migration. So can the movement of aphids when, having depleted the young leaves on one food plant, their offspring then fly onward to a different host plant, with no one aphid ever returning to where it started.

Dingle is an evolutionary biologist who studies insects. His definition is more intricate than Berger’s, citing those five features that distinguish migration from other forms of movement. They allow for the fact that, for example, aphids will become sensitive to blue light (from the sky) when it’s time for takeoff on their big journey, and sensitive to yellow light (reflected from tender young leaves) when it’s appropriate to land. Birds will fatten themselves with heavy feeding in advance of a long migrational flight. The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers towards understanding how evolution has produced them all.

Human behaviour, however, is having a detrimental impact on animal migration. The pronghorn, which resembles an antelope, though they are unrelated, is the fastest land mammal of the New World. One population, which spends the summer in the mountainous Grand Teton National Park of the western USA, follows a narrow route from its summer range in the mountains, across a river, and down onto the plains. Here they wait out the frozen months, feeding mainly on sagebrush blown clear of snow. These pronghorns are notable for the invariance of their migration route and the severity of its constriction at three bottlenecks. If they can’t pass through each of the three during their spring migration, they can’t reach their bounty of summer grazing; if they can’t pass through again in autumn, escaping south onto those windblown plains, they are likely to die trying to overwinter in the deep snow. Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land, where they can see and run. At one of the bottlenecks, forested hills rise to form a V, leaving a corridor of open ground only about 150 metres wide, filled with private homes. Increasing development is leading toward a crisis for the pronghorn, threatening to choke off their passageway.

Conservation scientists, along with some biologists and land managers within the USA’s National Park Service and other agencies, are now working to preserve migrational behaviours, not just species and habitats. A National Forest has recognised the path of the pronghorn, much of which passes across its land, as a protected migration corridor. But neither the Forest Service nor the Park Service can control what happens on private land at a bottleneck. And with certain other migrating species, the challenge is complicated further – by vastly greater distances traversed, more jurisdictions, more borders, more dangers along the way. We will require wisdom and resoluteness to ensure that migrating species can continue their journeying a while longer.

parts.passageHtml:
Great Migrations

Animal migration, however it is defined, is far more than just the movement of animals. It can loosely be described as travel that takes place at regular intervals – often in an annual cycle – that may involve many members of a species, and is rewarded only after a long journey. It suggests inherited instinct. The biologist Hugh Dingle has identified five characteristics that apply, in varying degrees and combinations, to all migrations. They are prolonged movements that carry animals outside familiar habitats; they tend to be linear, not zigzaggy; they involve special behaviours concerning preparation (such as overfeeding) and arrival; they demand special allocations of energy. And one more: migrating animals maintain an intense attentiveness to the greater mission, which keeps them undistracted by temptations and undeterred by challenges that would turn other animals aside.

An arctic tern, on its 20,000 km flight from the extreme south of South America to the Arctic circle, will take no notice of a nice smelly herring offered from a bird-watcher’s boat along the way. While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on. Why? The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of something we humans find admirable: larger purpose. In other words, it is determined to reach its destination. The bird senses that it can eat, rest and mate later. Right now it is totally focused on the journey; its undivided intent is arrival.

Reaching some gravelly coastline in the Arctic, upon which other arctic terns have converged, will serve its larger purpose as shaped by evolution: finding a place, a time, and a set of circumstances in which it can successfully hatch and rear offspring.

But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study. Joe! Berger, of the University of Montana, who works on the American pronghorn and other large terrestrial mammals, prefers what he calls a simple, practical definition suited to his beasts: ‘movements from a seasonal home area away to another home area and back again’. Generally the reason for such seasonal back-and-forth movement is to seek resources that aren’t available within a single area year-round.

But daily vertical movements by zooplankton in the ocean – upward by night to seek food, downward by day to escape predators – can also be considered migration. So can the movement of aphids when, having depleted the young leaves on one food plant, their offspring then fly onward to a different host plant, with no one aphid ever returning to where it started.

Dingle is an evolutionary biologist who studies insects. His definition is more intricate than Berger’s, citing those five features that distinguish migration from other forms of movement. They allow for the fact that, for example, aphids will become sensitive to blue light (from the sky) when it’s time for takeoff on their big journey, and sensitive to yellow light (reflected from tender young leaves) when it’s appropriate to land. Birds will fatten themselves with heavy feeding in advance of a long migrational flight. The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers towards understanding how evolution has produced them all.

Human behaviour, however, is having a detrimental impact on animal migration. The pronghorn, which resembles an antelope, though they are unrelated, is the fastest land mammal of the New World. One population, which spends the summer in the mountainous Grand Teton National Park of the western USA, follows a narrow route from its summer range in the mountains, across a river, and down onto the plains. Here they wait out the frozen months, feeding mainly on sagebrush blown clear of snow. These pronghorns are notable for the invariance of their migration route and the severity of its constriction at three bottlenecks. If they can’t pass through each of the three during their spring migration, they can’t reach their bounty of summer grazing; if they can’t pass through again in autumn, escaping south onto those windblown plains, they are likely to die trying to overwinter in the deep snow. Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land, where they can see and run. At one of the bottlenecks, forested hills rise to form a V, leaving a corridor of open ground only about 150 metres wide, filled with private homes. Increasing development is leading toward a crisis for the pronghorn, threatening to choke off their passageway.

Conservation scientists, along with some biologists and land managers within the USA’s National Park Service and other agencies, are now working to preserve migrational behaviours, not just species and habitats. A National Forest has recognised the path of the pronghorn, much of which passes across its land, as a protected migration corridor. But neither the Forest Service nor the Park Service can control what happens on private land at a bottleneck. And with certain other migrating species, the challenge is complicated further – by vastly greater distances traversed, more jurisdictions, more borders, more dangers along the way. We will require wisdom and resoluteness to ensure that migrating species can continue their journeying a while longer.

questionGroups.instruction: Questions 14–18Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?In boxes 14–18 on your answer sheet, writeTRUE if the statement agrees with the informationFALSE if the statement contradicts the informationNOT GIVEN if there is no information on this
questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 14–18

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 14–18 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questionGroups.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.prompt: Local gulls and migrating arctic terns behave in the same way when offered food.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Local gulls”, “migrating arctic terns”, “same way”, “offered food”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2: “An arctic tern... will take no notice of a nice smelly herring offered... While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi được cho thức ăn, chim nhạn Bắc Cực ngó lơ (take no notice) và tiếp tục bay, trong khi mòng biển địa phương lại lao xuống ăn ngấu nghiến (dive voraciously). Do đó, hành vi của hai loài là hoàn toàn trái ngược nhau, không phải giống nhau (same way).

=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.prompt: Experts’ definitions of migration tend to vary according to their area of study.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“Experts’ definitions”, “vary” (thay đổi/khác nhau), “according to their area of study” (theo lĩnh vực nghiên cứu)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: “But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn khẳng định các nhà sinh vật học định nghĩa về di cư khác nhau (define it differently = vary), một phần phụ thuộc vào loại động vật mà họ nghiên cứu (what sorts of animals they study = area of study). Thông tin này hoàn toàn khớp với câu hỏi.

=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: Very few experts agree that the movement of aphids can be considered migration.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Very few experts agree”, “movement of aphids”, “considered migration”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 5: “So can the movement of aphids... be considered migration.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn chỉ khẳng định sự di chuyển của rệp (aphids) cũng có thể được coi là di cư. Bài viết hoàn toàn không cung cấp thông tin về số lượng chuyên gia đồng ý hay phản đối nhận định này (very few experts agree hay nhiều).

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Aphids’ journeys are affected by changes in the light that they perceive.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Aphids’ journeys”, “affected by”, “changes in the light”, “perceive” (nhận thức/cảm nhận)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “...aphids will become sensitive to blue light (from the sky) when it's time for takeoff... and sensitive to yellow light (reflected from tender young leaves) when it's appropriate to land.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Hành trình của loài rệp chịu ảnh hưởng trực tiếp bởi ánh sáng: chúng nhạy cảm với ánh sáng xanh để cất cánh và nhạy cảm với ánh sáng vàng để hạ cánh. Việc thay đổi phản ứng theo các màu sắc ánh sáng khác nhau đồng nghĩa với việc hành trình của chúng bị tác động bởi sự thay đổi ánh sáng mà chúng cảm nhận được.

=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: Dingles aim is to distinguish between the migratory behaviours of different species.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Dingle's aim” (mục đích của Dingle), “distinguish” (phân biệt), “migratory behaviours of different species”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Định nghĩa của Dingle hướng sự chú ý vào điểm chung (shares with) giữa hiện tượng di cư của linh dương đầu bò và loài rệp để tìm ra cơ chế tiến hóa chung, chứ mục đích của ông không phải là để phân biệt (distinguish) sự khác nhau giữa các loài. Do đó câu hỏi đưa tin sai lệch so với bài đọc.

=> Đáp án: FALSE

questionGroups.instruction: Questions 19–22Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A–G, below.Write the correct letter in boxes 19–22 on your answer sheet.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
According to Dingle, migratory routes are likely to 19_______

To prepare for migration, animals are likely to 20_______

During migration, animals are unlikely to 21_______

Arctic terns illustrate migrating animals’ ability to 22_______

List of options

A. be discouraged by difficulties.

B. travel on open land where they can look out for predators.

C. eat more than they need for immediate purposes.

D. be repeated daily.

E. ignore distractions.

F. be governed by the availability of water.

G. follow a straight line.
questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 19–22

Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A–G, below.

Write the correct letter in boxes 19–22 on your answer sheet.

listOptions.label: travel on open land where they can look out for predators.
eat more than they need for immediate purposes.
be governed by the availability of water.

questionGroups.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.prompt:
According to Dingle, migratory routes are likely to
___________.

options.label: travel on open land where they can look out for predators.
eat more than they need for immediate purposes.
be governed by the availability of water.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “According to Dingle”, “migratory routes”, “likely to”
 follow a straight line.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “The biologist Hugh Dingle has identified five characteristics... they tend to be linear, not zigzaggy...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Theo Dingle, một trong các đặc điểm của tuyến đường di cư là chúng có xu hướng theo đường thẳng, không ngoằn ngoèo (linear, not zigzaggy). Cụm này đồng nghĩa với cụm “follow a straight line” (đáp án G).

=> Đáp án: G (follow a straight line)
questions.questionHtml:
According to Dingle, migratory routes are likely to 19_______

To prepare for migration, animals are likely to 20_______

During migration, animals are unlikely to 21_______

Arctic terns illustrate migrating animals’ ability to 22_______

List of options

A. be discouraged by difficulties.

B. travel on open land where they can look out for predators.

C. eat more than they need for immediate purposes.

D. be repeated daily.

E. ignore distractions.

F. be governed by the availability of water.

G. follow a straight line.

questions.prompt:
To prepare for migration, animals are likely to
___________.

options.label: travel on open land where they can look out for predators.
eat more than they need for immediate purposes.
be governed by the availability of water.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “To prepare for migration”, “animals are likely to”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “...they involve special behaviours concerning preparation (such as overfeeding) and arrival...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Hành vi đặc biệt liên quan đến việc chuẩn bị (preparation) được Dingle lấy ví dụ là ăn quá nhiều (overfeeding). Khái niệm này hoàn toàn khớp với đáp án C: “eat more than they need for immediate purposes” (ăn nhiều hơn mức nhu cầu phục vụ mục đích tức thời).

=> Đáp án: C(eat more than they need for immediate purposes)

questions.prompt:
During migration, animals are unlikely to
 ___________.

options.label: travel on open land where they can look out for predators.
eat more than they need for immediate purposes.
be governed by the availability of water.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “During migration”, “animals are unlikely to” (động vật không có khả năng/hiếm khi làm gì)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “...which keeps them undistracted by temptations and undeterred by challenges that would turn other animals aside.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Những động vật di cư tập trung cao độ vào sứ mệnh lớn lao nên chúng sẽ không bị nản lòng trước các thử thách (undeterred by challenges). Vì câu hỏi dùng cấu trúc phủ định “unlikely to”, ta chọn đáp án A: “be discouraged by difficulties” (bị nản lòng bởi những khó khăn) – vì undeterred đồng nghĩa với not discouraged.

=> Đáp án: A ( be discouraged by difficulties)

questions.prompt:
Arctic terns illustrate migrating animals’ ability to
 ___________

options.label: travel on open land where they can look out for predators.
eat more than they need for immediate purposes.
be governed by the availability of water.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“Arctic terns”, “illustrate migrating animals’ ability to” (minh họa cho khả năng...)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2: “The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of... larger purpose.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Loài chim nhạn Bắc Cực được đưa ra làm ví dụ chứng minh cho việc chúng có thể cưỡng lại các yếu tố gây xao nhãng (resists distraction) từ lời mời gọi thức ăn dọc đường để tiếp tục bay. Cụm này khớp hoàn toàn với đáp án E: “ignore distractions” (bỏ qua những sự xao nhãng).

=> Đáp án: E (ignore distractions)

questionGroups.instruction: Questions 23–26Complete the summary below.Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
The migration of pronghorns

Pronghorns rely on their eyesight and 23_______ to avoid predators. One particular population’s summer habitat is a national park, and their winter home is on the 24_______ where they go to avoid the danger presented by the snow at that time of year. However, their route between these two areas contains three 25_______ One problem is the construction of new homes in a narrow

26_______ corridor of land on the pronghorns’ route.
questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 23–26

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.noteContent:
Pronghorns rely on their eyesight and [23] to avoid predators. One particular population’s summer habitat is a national park, and their winter home is on the [24] where they go to avoid the danger presented by the snow at that time of year. However, their route between these two areas contains three [25] One problem is the construction of new homes in a narrow 
[26] corridor of land on the pronghorns’ route.

questions.prompt: Pronghorns rely on their eyesight and 23 .................. to avoid predators.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Pronghorns rely on”, “eyesight”, “avoid predators”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: “Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Để giữ an toàn trước kẻ săn mồi (keep safe from predators = avoid predators), loài linh dương sừng gai phụ thuộc vào tầm nhìn xa (distance vision = eyesight) và yếu tố còn lại là tốc độ — speed.

=> Đáp án: speed
questions.questionHtml:
The migration of pronghorns

Pronghorns rely on their eyesight and 23_______ to avoid predators. One particular population’s summer habitat is a national park, and their winter home is on the 24_______ where they go to avoid the danger presented by the snow at that time of year. However, their route between these two areas contains three 25_______ One problem is the construction of new homes in a narrow

26_______ corridor of land on the pronghorns’ route.

questions.prompt: One particular population’s summer habitat is a national park, and their winter home is on the 24 .................
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “winter home”, “avoid the danger presented by the snow”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: “...follows a narrow route from its summer range in the mountains... and down onto the plains. Here they wait out the frozen months, feeding mainly on sagebrush blown clear of snow.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Nơi chúng chọn làm nhà để đi xuống trú ẩn và vượt qua những tháng băng giá đông lạnh (wait out the frozen months = winter home) nhằm tránh tuyết dày chính là các vùng đồng bằng — plains.

=> Đáp án: plains

questions.prompt: However, their route between these two areas contains three
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “route between these two areas”, “contains three”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: “These pronghorn are notable for the invariance of their migration route and the severity of its construction at three bottlenecks.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tuyến đường di cư của chúng có đặc điểm là cực kỳ khắc nghiệt tại cấu trúc gồm có 3 điểm nghẽn giao thông — bottlenecks.

=> Đáp án: bottlenecks

questions.prompt: The wildebeest migration is the largest in the world.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “construction of new homes”, “narrow... of land”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: “...leaving a corridor of open ground only about 150 metres wide, filled with private homes.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Việc xây dựng các ngôi nhà riêng (private homes = new homes) xuất hiện dày đặc tại một dải đất trống hẹp chỉ rộng khoảng 150 mét. Danh từ chỉ dải đất/hành lang này là corridor (hoặc từ đồng nghĩa xuất hiện trong đoạn là passageway).

=> Đáp án: corridor

parts.rangeLabel: Read the text and answer questions 27–40

passage.text: Preface to ‘How the other half thinks: Adventures in mathematical reasoning’

parts.passageHtml:
Preface to ‘How the other half thinks: Adventures in mathematical reasoning’

Preface to ‘How the other half thinks: Adventures in mathematical reasoning’

A. Occasionally, in some difficult musical compositions, there are beautiful, but easy parts – parts so simple a beginner could play them. So it is with mathematics as well. There are some discoveries in advanced mathematics that do not depend on specialized knowledge, not even on algebra, geometry, or trigonometry. Instead they may involve, at most, a little arithmetic, such as ‘the sum of two odd numbers is even’, and common sense. Each of the eight chapters in this book illustrates this phenomenon. Anyone can understand every step in the reasoning.

The thinking in each chapter uses at most only elementary arithmetic, and sometimes not even that. Thus all readers will have the chance to participate in a mathematical experience, to appreciate the beauty of mathematics, and to become familiar with its logical, yet intuitive, style of thinking.


B. One of my purposes in writing this book is to give readers who haven’t had the opportunity to see and enjoy real mathematics the chance to appreciate the mathematical way of thinking. I want to reveal not only some of the fascinating discoveries, but, more importantly, the reasoning behind them.

In that respect, this book differs from most books on mathematics written for the general public. Some present the lives of colorful mathematicians. Others describe important applications of mathematics. Yet others go into mathematical procedures, but assume that the reader is adept in using algebra.


C. I hope this book will help bridge that notorious gap that separates the two cultures: the humanities and the sciences, or should I say the right brain (intuitive) and the left brain (analytical, numerical). As the chapters will illustrate, mathematics is not restricted to the analytical and numerical; intuition plays a significant role. The alleged gap can be narrowed or completely overcome by anyone, in part because each of us is far from using the full capacity of either side of the brain. To illustrate our human potential, I cite a structural engineer who is an artist, an electrical engineer who is an opera singer, an opera singer who published mathematical research, and a mathematician who publishes short stories.


D. Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics, although it provides the foundation of their theories. The reader must remain a tantalized spectator rather than an involved participant, since the appropriate language for describing the details in much of science is mathematics, whether the subject is expanding universe, subatomic particles, or chromosomes. Though the broad outline of a scientific theory can be sketched intuitively, when a part of the physical universe is finally understood, its description often looks like a page in a mathematics text.


E. Still, the non-mathematical reader can go far in understanding mathematical reasoning. This book presents the details that illustrate the mathematical style of thinking, which involves sustained, step-by-step analysis, experiments, and insights. You will turn these pages much more slowly than when reading a novel or a newspaper. It may help to have a pencil and paper ready to check claims and carry out experiments.


F. As I wrote, I kept in mind two types of readers: those who enjoyed mathematics until they were turned off by an unpleasant episode, usually around fifth grade, and mathematics aficionados, who will find much that is new throughout the book.

This book also serves readers who simply want to sharpen their analytical skills. Many careers, such as law and medicine, require extended, precise analysis. Each chapter offers practice in following a sustained and closely argued line of thought. That mathematics can develop this skill is shown by these two testimonials:


G. A physician wrote, ‘The discipline of analytical thought processes in mathematics prepared me extremely well for medical school. In medicine one is faced with a problem which must be thoroughly analyzed before a solution can be found. The process is similar to doing mathematics.’

A lawyer made the same point, “Although I had no background in law – not even one political science course — I did well at one of the best law schools. I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics, and, in particular, theorems, how to analyze complicated principles. Lawyers who have studied mathematics can master the legal principles in a way that most others cannot.’
I hope you will share my delight in watching as simple, even naive, questions lead to remarkable solutions and purely theoretical discoveries find unanticipated applications.

questionGroups.instruction: Questions 27–34Reading Passage has sections A–G. Which section contains the following information?Write the correct letter, A–G, in boxes 27–34 on your answer sheet.NB You may use any letter more than once.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
List of options

A. A

B. B

C. C

D. D

E. E

27_______  a reference to books that assume a lack of mathematical knowledge

28_______  the way in which this is not a typical book about mathematics

29_______  personal examples of being helped by mathematics

30_______  examples of people who each had abilities that seemed incompatible

31_______  mention of different focuses of books about mathematics

32_______  a contrast between reading this book and reading other kinds of publication

33_______  a claim that the whole of the book is accessible to everybody

34_______  a reference to different categories of intended readers of this book
questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 27–34

Reading Passage has sections A–G. Which section contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A–G, in boxes 27–34 on your answer sheet.

NB You may use any letter more than once.
questionGroups.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.prompt: a reference to books that assume a lack of mathematical knowledge
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “reference to books”, “assume a lack of mathematical knowledge” (giả định người đọc thiếu kiến thức toán)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D: “Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn D nhắc tới việc các nhà khoa học khác viết sách cho đối tượng là những "nhà phi khoa học" (non-scientists), điều này đồng nghĩa với việc họ giả định nhóm độc giả này không có/thiếu nền tảng kiến thức toán học chuyên sâu nên đã chủ động lược bỏ phần toán đi (omit the mathematics).

=> Đáp án: D

questions.prompt: the way in which this is not a typical book about mathematics
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “not a typical book” (không phải một cuốn sách điển hình/thông thường)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “In that respect, this book differs from most books on mathematics written for the general public.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả khẳng định cuốn sách này có sự khác biệt so với phần lớn các sách toán học thông thường dành cho đại chúng (differs from most books = not a typical book) ở chỗ nó chú trọng bóc tách phần lập luận phân tích phía sau các khám phá nghệ thuật.

=> Đáp án: B

questions.prompt: personal examples of being helped by mathematics
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “personal examples” (ví dụ cá nhân), “being helped by mathematics” (được giúp đỡ bởi toán học)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn G: “A physician wrote... prepared me extremely well for medical school... A lawyer made the same point... I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn G trích dẫn lời chứng thực trực tiếp từ một bác sĩ và một luật sư kể về việc tư duy toán học đã giúp ích lớn như thế nào cho quá trình học tập và làm nghề thực tế của chính họ.

=> Đáp án: G

questions.prompt: examples of people who each had abilities that seemed incompatible
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “abilities that seemed incompatible” (những năng lực/khả năng có vẻ trái ngược, không tương thích nhau)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C: “To illustrate our human potential, I cite a structural engineer who is an artist, an electrical engineer who is an opera singer, an opera singer who published mathematical research, and a mathematician who publishes short stories.”  

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả liệt kê một loạt ví dụ về các cá nhân sở hữu song song hai năng lực thuộc hai phạm trù hoàn toàn đối lập (não trái phân tích lý tính và não phải nghệ thuật trực giác) như: kỹ sư điện làm ca sĩ opera, nhà toán học viết truyện ngắn.  

=> Đáp án: C

questions.prompt: mention of different focuses of books about mathematics
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “different focuses of books” (những trọng tâm/hướng tiếp cận khác nhau của các sách về toán)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “Some present the lives of colorful mathematicians. Others describe important applications of mathematics. Yet others go into mathematical procedures...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả liệt kê các lối khai thác đề tài khác nhau của sách toán học trên thị trường: cuốn thì tập trung viết về cuộc đời nhà toán học (lives), cuốn tập trung vào tính ứng dụng (applications), cuốn lại đi sâu vào quy trình giải toán (procedures).

=> Đáp án: B

questions.prompt: a contrast between reading this book and reading other kinds of publication
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “contrast” (sự tương phản/so sánh đối lập), “reading other kinds of publication”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn E: “You will turn these pages much more slowly than when reading a novel or a newspaper.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả chỉ ra sự tương phản rõ rệt khi đọc cuốn sách này là bạn sẽ phải lật từng trang chậm hơn rất nhiều (turn these pages much more slowly) so với tốc độ đọc các loại ấn phẩm giải trí thông thường khác như tiểu thuyết (novel) hay báo chí (newspaper).

=> Đáp án: E

questions.prompt: a claim that the whole of the book is accessible to everybody
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “the whole of the book” (toàn bộ cuốn sách), “accessible to everybody” (ai cũng có thể tiếp cận/hiểu được)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn A: “Each of the eight chapters in this book illustrates this phenomenon. Anyone can understand every step in the reasoning.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Câu văn khẳng định tất cả 8 chương trong sách (Each of the eight chapters = the whole of the book) đều được viết sao cho bất kỳ ai cũng có thể hiểu được từng bước lập luận (Anyone can understand = accessible to everybody).

=> Đáp án: A

questions.prompt: a reference to different categories of intended readers of this book
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “different categories of intended readers” (các nhóm độc giả mục tiêu khác nhau)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn F: “As I wrote, I kept in mind two types of readers: those who enjoyed mathematics until they were turned off... and mathematics aficionados...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả chỉ rõ hai nhóm đối tượng độc giả (two types of readers = different categories of readers) mà ông hướng tới khi viết cuốn sách này: một là những người từng sợ toán sau biến cố ở trường học, và hai là những người vốn đã yêu thích và đam mê toán học (aficionados).

=> Đáp án: F

questionGroups.instruction: Questions 35–40Complete the sentences below.Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.blockHtml:
• Some areas of both music and mathematics are suitable for someone who is a 35_______

• It is sometimes possible to understand advanced mathematics using no more than a limited knowledge of 36_______

• The writer intends to show that mathematics requires 37_______ thinking, as well as analytical skills.

• Some books written by 38_______ have had to leave out the mathematics that is central to their theories.

• The writer advises non-mathematical readers to perform 39_______ while reading.

• A lawyer found that studying 40_______ helped even more than other areas of mathematics in the study of law.
questionGroups.groupHtml:
Questions 35–40

Complete the sentences below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questionGroups.noteContent:
• Some areas of both music and mathematics are suitable for someone who is a [35] 
• It is sometimes possible to understand advanced mathematics using no more than a limited knowledge of [36] 
• The writer intends to show that mathematics requires [37]  thinking, as well as analytical skills.
• Some books written by [38] have had to leave out the mathematics that is central to their theories.
• The writer advises non-mathematical readers to perform [39] while reading.
• A lawyer found that studying [40] helped even more than other areas of mathematics in the study of law.

questions.prompt: Some areas of both music and mathematics are suitable for someone who is a _______ .
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “areas of both music and mathematics”, “suitable for someone who is a”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn A: “Occasionally, in some difficult musical compositions, there are beautiful, but easy parts - parts so simple a beginner could play them. So it is with mathematics as well.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn so sánh âm nhạc và toán học đều có những phần vô cùng đơn giản, dễ dàng tới mức một người mới bắt đầu chơi hay mới học cũng có thể xử lý được. Từ đơn thích hợp điền vào vị trí danh từ sau mạo từ "a" là beginner.

=> Đáp án: beginner
questions.questionHtml:
• Some areas of both music and mathematics are suitable for someone who is a 35_______

• It is sometimes possible to understand advanced mathematics using no more than a limited knowledge of 36_______

• The writer intends to show that mathematics requires 37_______ thinking, as well as analytical skills.

• Some books written by 38_______ have had to leave out the mathematics that is central to their theories.

• The writer advises non-mathematical readers to perform 39_______ while reading.

• A lawyer found that studying 40_______ helped even more than other areas of mathematics in the study of law.

questions.prompt: It is sometimes possible to understand advanced mathematics using no more than a limited knowledge of _______ .
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “advanced mathematics”, “using no more than” (không quá/nhiều nhất là), “limited knowledge of”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn A: “There are some discoveries in advanced mathematics that do not depend on specialized knowledge... Instead, they may involve, at most, a little arithmetic...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Để hiểu được một số khám phá toán cao cấp, người đọc không cần kiến thức chuyên sâu mà chỉ cần nhiều nhất là một chút kiến thức số học sơ cấp (at most, a little arithmetic tương đồng với no more than a limited knowledge of arithmetic).

=> Đáp án: arithmetic

questions.prompt: The writer intends to show that mathematics requires _______ thinking, as well as analytical skills.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “requires _______ thinking”, “as well as analytical skills”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C: “...mathematics is not restricted to the analytical and numerical; intuition plays a significant role... or should I say the right brain (intuitive) and the left brain (analytical, numerical).”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả nhấn mạnh toán học không chỉ giới hạn ở kỹ năng phân tích số liệu tính toán của não trái (analytical skills), mà nó còn đòi hỏi vai trò lớn của trực giác thuộc về não phải (intuition / intuitive). Do đó tính từ bổ nghĩa cho tư duy ở đây là intuitive.

=> Đáp án: intuitive

questions.prompt: Some books written by _______ have had to leave out the mathematics that is central to their theories.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “books written by”, “leave out the mathematics” (bỏ phần toán đi)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D: “Other scientists have written books to explain their fields to non-scientists, but have necessarily had to omit the mathematics...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “omit” đồng nghĩa với cụm cụm từ “leave out”. Và chủ thể viết ra những cuốn sách buộc phải lược bỏ toán học này chính là các nhà khoa học — scientists.

=> Đáp án: scientists

questions.prompt: The writer advises non-mathematical readers to perform _______ while reading.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “advises non-mathematical readers”, “perform _______ while reading”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn E: “It may help to have a pencil and paper ready to check claims and carry out experiments.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả đưa ra lời khuyên cho người đọc không chuyên là nên chuẩn bị sẵn giấy bút để kiểm tra các khẳng định và tiến hành/thực hiện các thử nghiệm. Động từ “carry out” đồng nghĩa với động từ “perform”, do đó danh từ cần điền là experiments.

=> Đáp án: experiments

questions.prompt: A lawyer found that studying _______ helped even more than other areas of mathematics in the study of law.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “A lawyer found”, “studying _______ helped even more”, “other areas of mathematics”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn G: “I attribute much of my success there to having learned, through the study of mathematics, and, in particular, theorems, how to analyze complicated principles.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Người luật sư chia sẻ rằng ông gặt hái được thành công nhờ việc học toán, và nhấn mạnh một phần cụ thể đặc biệt quan trọng (in particular tương đồng với helped even more than other areas) chính là việc học các định lý — theorems.

=> Đáp án: theorems
